sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The top-center cell (0, 1) changed from white to green! So painting a pattern cell with the cursor at the right end of the long segment changes it from white to green.
Current grid:
- Top row: g, N, g (top-center is now green)
- Middle row: W, g, W
- Bottom row: g, W, g
So the pattern cells can be painted with different colors depending on the cursor position:
- Cursor at right end of long segment: paint white (W)
- Cursor at right end of long segment (again?): paint green (N)
Wait, the cursor was at the same position (cols 39-42) when I painted before. But earlier it painted white, now it painted green. Maybe the paint color depends on the current cell color:
- If cell is gray, paint white
- If cell is white, paint green
- If cell is green, paint gray (reset)
This would be a toggle mechanism: gray -> white -> green -> gray -> ...
Or maybe the paint color depends on something else.
Let me continue painting the remaining pattern cells to green and see if the level completes.
Pattern cells:
- (0, 1): already green
- (1, 0): white -> paint to green
- (1, 2): white -> paint to green
- (2, 1): white -> paint to green

<parameter=code>
# Paint remaining pattern cells to green
# (1,0) middle-left, (1,2) middle-right, (2,1) bottom-center
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},  # (1,0)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},  # (1,2)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},  # (2,1)
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
